entities.name: BEAN
entities.description: The primary crop used to analyze the productivity gap between male and female producers.

entities.name: GENDER YIELD GAP
entities.description: The difference in agricultural productivity between male and female farmers.

entities.name: TANZANIA
entities.description: The country where the study on bean producers was conducted.

entities.name: SOCIAL NORMS
entities.description: Societal expectations and rules that contribute to the invisibility of women in agriculture.

entities.name: NATIONAL AGRICULTURAL POLICY
entities.description: The government's policy framework for agriculture, which often overlooks female farmers.

entities.name: ACCESS TO EDUCATION
entities.description: The availability of educational opportunities, which affects the productivity of female farmers.

entities.name: ACCESS TO TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: The availability of agricultural tools and technology, which affects the productivity of female farmers.

entities.name: TRAINING
entities.description: The provision of agricultural knowledge and skills to farmers.

entities.name: LAND RIGHTS
entities.description: The legal and social rights to own or use land, affecting female productivity.

entities.name: NUTRITIOUS FOOD CROPS
entities.description: Crops that provide essential nutrients, often a priority for female farmers.

entities.name: VARIETAL SELECTION
entities.description: The process of choosing specific crop varieties based on desired attributes.

entities.name: HOUSEHOLD FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food.

entities.name: HOME STORAGE
entities.description: The practice of storing crops at the household level.

entities.name: SMALL-SCALE PROCESSING
entities.description: The processing of agricultural products on a small scale, often ignored in policy design.

entities.name: THREE-STAGE STRATIFIED SAMPLING
entities.description: A sampling method used to collect cross-sectional data across different regions.

entities.name: OAXACA-BLINDER DECOMPOSITION
entities.description: A statistical method used to apportion the sources of productivity differences between two groups.

entities.name: RE-CENTERED INFLUENCE FUNCTIONS
entities.description: A method used to analyze differences through unconditional partial effects on quantiles.

entities.name: COUNTERFACTUAL APPROACH
entities.description: A modeling approach used to provide consistent estimates for group comparison by simulating alternative scenarios.

entities.name: SELECTION BIAS
entities.description: A systematic error introduced when the sample is not representative of the population.

entities.name: IMPROVED BEAN SEED VARIETIES
entities.description: High-yielding or enhanced bean varieties that increase productivity.

entities.name: GOOD AGRICULTURAL PRACTICES
entities.description: Standardized farming methods used to improve crop yield and quality.

entities.name: INDIGENOUS VARIETIES
entities.description: Local or traditional bean varieties used by farmers.

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of agricultural output per unit of input.
